Undergraduate and graduate departments include the Department of Information and Communication Engineering, the Department of International Finance and Management, the Department of Agriculture and Life Sciences, the Department of Medicine, and the Graduate School of Dentistry.

question: Okryu-gwan, located on the banks of the Taedong River in Pyongyang, North Korea, is a famous North Korean restaurant to the extent that it has branches not only in Pyongyang but also overseas. Write the name of the representative food of Pyongyang, where buckwheat noodles and radish water kimchi broth harmonize, which is the representative menu of the Pyongyang Okryu-gwan restaurant.
explanation: Okryu-gwan, located on the banks of the Taedong River in Pyongyang, is a representative public restaurant and state banquet hall in North Korea that opened in 1960. It is characterized by its traditional-style blue tiled roof, and is famous for its Pyongyang Naengmyeon, as well as meat tray noodles (Gogi-jaengban-guksu) and sturgeon dishes. It is also well-known as a symbolic place for South-North reconciliation, as major foreign guests visited at the time of the inter-Korean summits. Recently, it has been leading the dining-out culture of Pyongyang citizens by introducing modern equipment and strengthening delivery services.

question: As a railway connecting Seoul and Sinuiju, the northernmost station of the current South Korean section is Dorasan Station. In 2007, freight trains operated regularly between Dorasan Station in the South and Bongdong Station in the North. What is the name of this railway line?
explanation: The Gyeongui Line is a major longitudinal railway of the Korean Peninsula connecting Seoul and Sinuiju. Restoration projects were pursued according to the agreement of the 2000 Inter-Korean Summit; in the South, operations to Dorasan Station began in 2002, and in 2007, freight trains operated regularly for about 11 months in the section between Dorasan Station in the South and Bongdong Station in the North. Currently, the South Korean section is only operating to Dorasan Station within the Civilian Control Zone (CCZ), and this place has become a place symbolizing the aspiration for overcoming the division of the Korean Peninsula and unification under the slogan “Not the last station from the South, but the first station toward the North.” The Gyeongui Line is expected to become a core axis of the peaceful economy of the Korean Peninsula by connecting with the Trans-Chinese Railway (TCR), etc., in the future.

question: Write the word for donut in North Korea.
explanation: North Korea replaces loanwords with Korean words through a language purification policy. Donuts are called ‘Garakji-ppang’ after their shape (ring-shaped bread), and ramen is called ‘Kkoburang-guksu’ (curly noodles) after its curly noodles. Cup noodles are called ‘Jeukseok-guksu’ (instant noodles) in the sense that they can be cooked and eaten instantly.

question: Write the name of the representative newspaper of North Korea that represents North Korea’s position on major domestic and foreign current issues or events as the organ of the Workers’ Party of Korea.
explanation: Rodong Sinmun is the organ of the Central Committee of the Workers’ Party of Korea and is the most authoritative daily newspaper in North Korea. It was founded in 1945 under the name ‘Jeongro’ and is used as a core medium to convey the party’s line and policies to the people and to educate them in ideology. Recently, in line with the digitalization trend, it has been delivering news abroad through its website. It serves as an important scale for gauging domestic and foreign policy directions.

question: Write the railway corresponding to ( A ). If the Gyeongui Line and Donghae Line are restored and a railway track starting from the Korean Peninsula and leading to Europe via China, Russia, Mongolia, etc., is connected, logistics costs can be reduced, and as the railroad is connected, tourists can freely come and go. This refers to the ( A ), a railway connecting the South and the North, being linked with the Trans-Siberian Railway (TSR) and the Trans-Chinese Railway (TCR). To achieve this, exchange and cooperation between the South and the North are more important than anything else.
explanation: If the Trans-Korean Railway (TKR) is connected by restoring the Gyeongui and Donghae Lines, it can be linked with the Trans-Siberian Railway (TSR) and the Trans-Chinese Railway (TCR), allowing logistics and tourists to move freely by train. Additionally, in the case of maritime transport, it takes about 30–40 days to reach Europe, and if one has to go around the Cape of Good Hope at the southern tip of Africa due to being unable to pass through the Suez Canal, it can take more than 60 days. On the other hand, using the railway allows for transport within approximately 10–15 days, significantly shortening the transport period.

question: It is said that if the Korean Peninsula railway is connected, this city can grow into the largest logistics base connecting the Eurasian continent and the Pacific Ocean over 55 million square kilometers. As a port city located at the southeastern tip of the Korean Peninsula and currently the world’s 5th largest trade port based on container cargo volume in 2017, where is this city?
explanation: If the Korean Peninsula railway is connected in the future, Busan will play the role of the largest logistics base connecting the Eurasian continent and the Pacific Ocean.

question: It is said that mineral resources are buried in 80% of North Korea’s land, and this place, the largest open-pit mine in Asia, is also said to have more than 2 to 3 billion tons of iron buried. Write the name of this place, where POSCO of South Korea tried to promote joint development between the South and the North immediately after the October 4, 2007 Inter-Korean Summit.
explanation: The Musan Iron Mine is North Korea’s largest iron mine, located in Musan County, North Hamgyong Province. It is known as one of the largest open-pit (露天) iron mines in Asia and is evaluated to have iron ore deposits of more than 2 to 3 billion tons. In the atmosphere of expanding inter-Korean economic cooperation after the 2007 October 4 Inter-Korean Summit, POSCO, a South Korean steel company, reviewed cooperation plans utilizing North Korea’s iron ore resources. POSCO discussed plans with the North to import iron ore from the Musan Iron Mine or participate in mine development and even envisioned an industrial cooperation model linking iron ore mining and steelmaking by constructing a steel mill in the Musan area in the long term.

question: The entire area of the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) has been designated as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Write O if correct, and X if incorrect.
explanation: In June 2019, the border areas of Gangwon Province (Cheorwon-gun, Hwacheon-gun, Yanggu-gun, Inje-gun, Goseong-gun) excluding Paju in Gyeonggi-do, and the area around the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) such as Yeoncheon-gun in Gyeonggi-do, were designated as UNESCO Biosphere Reserves. Paju City also pushed for an application for UNESCO Biosphere Reserve designation in 2011, but in 2018, it did not apply due to economic development such as the creation of an industrial complex and was excluded, so it proceeded only with Yeoncheon and Gangwon Province. It was reserved during the deliberation process of the UNESCO International Coordinating Council of the Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Program in 2012 but was designated in 2019. UNESCO’s major designation systems include World Heritage which protects cultural and natural heritage, Biosphere Reserves which aim for ecosystem conservation and sustainable use, Global Geoparks which protect areas with outstanding geological value, Memory of the World which targets records and documents to be preserved by humanity, and Intangible Cultural Heritage which protects living cultures such as traditions, customs, and arts.

question: What is the concept corresponding to the following description? This is a special zone for promoting industrial and tourism-type development projects to realize a South-North economic community within the border region. Based on a basic plan established by the Ministry of Unification in December 2025, it is scheduled to be promoted step-by-step from 2026 to 2027 by selecting among 17 city and county border regions including Incheon (Ganghwa, Ongjin), Gyeonggi (Gimpo, Paju, Yeoncheon, Goyang, Dongducheon, Yangju, Pocheon, Gapyeong), and Gangwon (Goseong, Yanggu, Inje, Hwacheon, Cheorwon, Chuncheon, Sokcho). It aims to achieve balanced development of the land by developing border regions that have been excluded from development due to division, and in the medium to long term, to contribute to creating a South-North economic community by inducing domestic industrial production and forming a virtuous cycle mechanism for South-North economic growth.
explanation: The Peace Economic Special Zone aims to contribute to the formation of a South-North economic community and is scheduled to be promoted by selecting and designating among 17 border cities and counties. The Peace Economic Special Zone is a project providing tax and levy reductions, regulatory exceptions, and infrastructure support for the development of border regions and the activation of inter-Korean exchange and cooperation industries. In December 2025, the Ministry of Unification established the 1st Basic Plan for Peace Economic Special Zones through ▷listening to opinions of local governments, ▷consultation with relevant ministries, and ▷research commissions. Accordingly, the Ministry of Unification and the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport plan to receive development plans from local governments for the designation of Peace Economic Special Zones twice in 2026 and 2027.

question: Which of the following is NOT an event for which the South and the North formed a single team in an international sports competition?
explanation: In table tennis, a single team was first formed at the 1991 World Table Tennis Championships and won a gold medal, and in basketball, a single team was first formed at the 2018 Jakarta-Palembang Asian Games and won a silver medal. In soccer, a single team was formed at the 1991 FIFA World Youth Championship and advanced to the quarterfinals.

question: Dorasan Station is the northernmost station on the Gyeongui Line located within the Civilian Control Zone in Paju, Gyeonggi-do. It is famous for the slogan “Not the last station from the South, but the first station toward the North,” and is a place symbolizing the division of the Korean Peninsula and the aspiration for unification. Write O if correct, and X if incorrect.
explanation: Dorasan Station, built on April 11, 2002, is the northernmost station on the Gyeongui Line located in the Civilian Control Zone in Paju, Gyeonggi-do. In fact, it is a symbolic station built with inter-Korean connection in mind rather than being the final stop of general railway operations. This station emphasizes the meaning that it is the starting point of unification and connection rather than the end of division through the slogan “Not the last station from the South, but the first station toward the North.” In fact, if inter-Korean railway connection is achieved in the future, Dorasan Station will play the role of a gateway leading to the continent via North Korea.

question: When going on a Mt. Kumgang tour from South Korea, one could also travel by private car. Write O if correct, and X if incorrect.
explanation: Mt. Kumgang tourism began operations in November 1998 in the form of maritime tourism using cruise ships. Later, as the road connecting the South and the North was opened in 2003, overland tourism via tour buses, etc., was carried out. Starting from March 2008, it developed into Mt. Kumgang tourism via private cars. However, Mt. Kumgang tourism was suspended after a tourist was shot and killed by a North Korean soldier in the early morning of July 11, 2008, within the North Korean zone of Jangjeon Port.

question: The Mt. Kumgang tourism project was carried out for about 10 years from its start in 1998 until its suspension in 2008, and about 1.95 million South Korean tourists visited. Write O if correct, and X if incorrect.
explanation: According to Hyundai Asan’s estimate, the number of South Korean tourists who visited Mt. Kumgang from the start of the Mt. Kumgang tourism project in 1998 until its suspension in 2008 is approximately 1,956,000. Mt. Kumgang tourism brought direct effects such as national economic effects through increased tourists and sales, the effect of activating Gangwon Province’s regional economy during the Mt. Kumgang visit process, and the effect of improving the tourism balance, as well as indirect effects such as reducing the burden of foreign debt repayment through enhanced national credit, expanding foreign investment attraction, and inducing employment.

question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding the Masikryong Ski Resort in Gangwon Province, North Korea?

choices.text: It was used as a joint training site for winter sports athletes of inter-Korean single teams.
It is a representative winter tourist destination developed to attract international ski competitions and tourists.
The South and the North also conducted joint training for military purposes.
It is evaluated as a symbolic area for inter-Korean sports exchange and peace cooperation.

explanation: The Masikryong Ski Resort, which opened in December 2013, is a representative winter tourist destination in Gangwon Province, North Korea, and a place for inter-Korean sports exchange. Joint training for inter-Korean ski athletes was conducted to prepare for the single team at the 2018 Pyeongchang Olympics, and it is evaluated as a symbolic place for peace cooperation through tourism and sports exchange. However, the South and the North have never conducted joint training for military purposes.

question: Which of the following is NOT a project conducted jointly by historians from the South and the North?

choices.text: Dangun Tomb excavation and restoration project in Pyongyang
Manwoldae joint excavation project in Kaesong
Investigation project for the excavation and repatriation of the remains of Patriot Ahn Jung-geun in Lushun
Joint status survey on Goguryeo tombs in the Pyongyang area

explanation: The Dangun Tomb excavation and restoration project in Pyongyang was pushed independently by North Korea in 1994.

question: After the 2018 Inter-Korean Summit, our government sent 200 tons of Jeju tangerines to North Korea as a gift. Write O if correct, and X if incorrect.
explanation: During the period when inter-Korean relations were improving in 2018, our government sent 200 tons of Jeju tangerines to the North Korean government in return for its gift of 2 tons of wild pine mushrooms. This is an example achieved as part of symbolic inter-Korean exchange. Meanwhile, even before 2018, activities of sending tangerines to North Korea had continued at the private and public levels. Since 1998, Jeju Island and Jeju regional organizations had conducted continuous private exchange by supporting North Korea with tens to hundreds of tons of tangerines annually for humanitarian and exchange purposes, but this is currently suspended.

question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding efforts for peace settlement through sociocultural exchange between South and North Korea?

choices.text: South-North sociocultural exchange began from the home-visiting of separated families in 1985.
During the home-visiting of separated families in 1985, mutual accompanying visit performances by South and North art troupes were carried out.
So far, reunions of separated families between South and North Korea have been conducted only in a face-to-face format.
Video reunions were introduced starting in 2005 due to the aging of separated families.

explanation: Sociocultural exchange between South and North Korea is also an important part of efforts to settle peace on the Korean Peninsula. South-North sociocultural exchange began with the home-visiting of separated families in 1985, and art troupes from the South and the North also accompanied each other for visiting performances, becoming the starting point of sociocultural exchange. On the Korean Peninsula, the issue of separated families is a humanitarian issue that transcends the political ideologies and systems of the South and the North, and is an important link in inter-Korean exchange and cooperation that fundamentally raises the necessity of peace on the Korean Peninsula. Between South and North Korea, from 1985 to 2018, a total of 21 face-to-face reunions and 7 video reunions were held, and there are cases conducted through mail. They were mainly conducted at the Mt. Kumgang Reunion Center for Separated Families and the Gaegumgang Hotel located in the Mt. Kumgang area. Video reunions were introduced starting from 2005 due to the aging of separated families, but face-to-face reunions have not been conducted since the last one in 2018.

question: Write the words to go into ( A ) and ( B ), respectively. • “If we transform the cost of division caused by hostility into a growth engine based on peace, we can convert the ‘Korea ( A )’ into ‘Korea ( B ).’” (Speech by President Lee Jae-myung, Chairperson, at the Inaugural Meeting of the 22nd National Unification Advisory Council) • Korea ( A ) refers to a phenomenon where investors perceive geopolitical risks such as the possibility of war due to division and political and security instability, along with institutional factors such as backward corporate governance, as risk factors and thus undervalue the assets of South Korea in advance. • Korea ( B ) refers to a situation where economic value is highly evaluated as peace and stability are settled on the Korean Peninsula or the investment environment is improved through manufacturing innovation, etc.
explanation: The phenomenon where South Korean companies and the South Korean stock market are evaluated low compared to their actual economic power or corporate performance is called ‘Korean Peninsula Risk’ or ‘Korea Risk.’ This is because concerns about geopolitical risks such as the possibility of war due to division or political and security instability, and the institutional environment such as backward corporate governance, are judged as risk factors by investors, leading them to undervalue South Korean assets in advance. Conversely, ‘Korea Premium’ refers to a situation where peace and stability are settled on the Korean Peninsula or the investment environment such as corporate operation and manufacturing innovation is improved, and economic value is highly evaluated.

question: Which region is the representative inter-Korean economic cooperation project where the following took place? • It is a North Korean economic special zone jointly established by the South and the North. • It was promoted based on the agreement between Hyundai Asan and North Korea in August 2000, began full operation in 2004, and was completely suspended in 2016.
explanation: The Kaesong Industrial Complex is an economic special zone in North Korea created through a joint venture between the South and the North. It was promoted following the agreement between Hyundai Asan and North Korea in August 2000, operated in 2004, and was completely suspended in 2016. During its operation, about 125 South Korean companies moved into the complex to produce products, and it is evaluated that more than about 55,000 North Korean workers were employed. According to the announcement by the Ministry of Unification, the total cumulative production value reached approximately 3.2 billion USD (about 3.5 trillion KRW). As such, the Kaesong Industrial Complex is significant in that it created jobs directly and indirectly through the employment of South and North Korean workers, sought to reduce production costs for South Korean companies by utilizing North Korean human resources, and served as a symbol of inter-Korean economic cooperation.

question: The Kaesong Industrial Complex project is an inter-Korean economic cooperation project promoted by combining our capital and technology with North Korea’s land and manpower. Write O if correct, and X if incorrect.
explanation: The Kaesong Industrial Complex project, which symbolizes inter-Korean cooperation, began with the signing of an agreement between Hyundai Asan and North Korea in August 2000. After holding a groundbreaking ceremony on June 30, 2003, factories began operating and producing products on December 15, 2004. At that time, there were about 50,000 North Korean workers, and the number of South Korean company employees was at the level of 1,000. It has been in a state of complete suspension since February 2016 after repeated suspensions and resumptions depending on the political situation.

question: Write the name of this economic special zone located in the area of Rajin City and Sonbong County in North Hamgyong Province, North Korea, which is a strategic economic zone equipped with logistics infrastructure such as ports and railways. It was designated by the North Korean government to attract foreign investment.
explanation: The Rajin-Sonbong Free Trade Zone was designated as North Korea’s first free economic and trade zone in December 1991 and is an economic special zone located in the area of Rajin City and Sonbong County in North Hamgyong Province, North Korea, which is a strategic economic zone equipped with logistics infrastructure such as ports and railways. It was designated by the North Korean government for the purpose of attracting foreign investment and promoting exports and imports, and is evaluated as a potential base for inter-Korean economic cooperation and northern economic linkage. Its main purpose is to promote economic activation by attracting foreign investment and to build an industrial complex development and logistics-centered economic structure centered on ports and railways. Furthermore, it also has the strategic meaning of activating the linked economy of the South, the North, and the North through the linkage of trade and logistics networks with northern countries such as Russia and China. However, large-scale investment and industrial complex development were limited due to North Korea’s nuclear issue and international sanctions, and there is a limitation in that the participation of South and North Korean companies was also realistically limited.

question: Write the term referring to the costs we must bear due to problems caused by division like the following. • Humanitarian issues such as confirmation of life/death, reunion, and reintegration of separated families • Excessive national defense expenditure issues due to the military confrontation between South and North Korea • Geopolitical issues of being part of the continent but unable to advance into the continent by land • Diplomatic issues of having to compete wastefully in the international community
explanation: Division costs is a collective term referring to the burdens we must bear as long as the South-North division continues. Division costs include visible tangible costs and intangible costs that are difficult to confirm directly by eye. Tangible costs include security costs required for national defense, diplomatic costs, and system maintenance costs, while intangible costs refer to costs associated with social problems such as fear of war, the suffering of separated families, ideological conflict, and restrictions on land use and unbalanced development; these are also called social costs.

question: Which of the following is NOT a unification cost?

choices.text: Improvement costs for roads, railways, and waterways on the Korean Peninsula

explanation: Unification costs refer to the economic and non-economic costs that must be spent after unification to form a single system for the South and the North. They include improvement costs for roads, railways, and waterways on the Korean Peninsula, economic (corporate) activation support costs (regional economic activation, support for short-distance transport such as subways), social security costs (pension, childcare, labor market subsidies), congestion costs in housing, transport, and education due to population movement, and social confusion costs. However, military costs are generally costs used to maintain national security and military power, and are not included in the category of unification costs occurring during the social and economic integration process after unification. Rather, military costs are included in the division costs that occur when maintaining division.

question: Write the term meaning the totality of economic and non-economic rewards and benefits that unification will bring.
explanation: Tangible benefits of unification benefits include the reduction of national defense expenditure, resolution of diplomatic competition costs, expansion of economic scale, and increased complementarity of South and North economies, while intangible benefits include the resolution of the separated families issue, disappearance of the possibility of war, improvement of international status, growth of autonomy, expansion of living radius, and expansion of diversified culture.

question: The name of the division line currently dividing the South and the North is called the ‘38th Parallel.’ Write O if correct, and X if incorrect.
explanation: According to the Armistice Agreement on July 27, 1953, the name of the division line dividing the South and the North was designated as the ‘Military Demarcation Line (MDL)’ or ‘Truce Line.’

question: The girl group Red Velvet has performed in the North as a South Korean art troupe. Write O if correct, and X if incorrect.
explanation: On April 1, 2018, at the East Pyongyang Grand Theatre and on April 3 at the Ryugyong Chung Ju-yung Gymnasium, performances in Pyongyang by the South Korean art troupe praying for South-North peace and cooperation were held. In the performances held under the subtitle “Spring is Coming,” major South Korean singers and idol groups such as Red Velvet, Cho Yong-pil, Lee Sun-hee, Yoon Do-hyun (YB), Baek Ji-young, and Seohyun (Girls’ Generation) participated. The performances were conducted in front of a North Korean audience and Chairman of the State Affairs Commission Kim Jong-un, contributing to sociocultural exchange and the creation of a peace atmosphere between the South and the North.

question: They are essential for high-tech industries such as smartphones, electric vehicle batteries, semiconductors, and wind turbines, and are called ‘vitamins of the future industry.’ Write the name of the strategic mineral resources that are known to have large deposits in the North Korean region and can increase South Korea’s future industrial competitiveness after the improvement of inter-Korean relations.
explanation: Rare earth elements are 17 rare metal elements essential for high-tech industries, and because their supply is concentrated in specific countries, they are classified as strategic resources. North Korea is known to have abundant rare earth deposits, centered around the North Hamgyong and Ryanggang Provinces, and according to some studies, potential deposits are estimated at about 20 million to over 200 million tons. North Korea’s rare earth elements are evaluated as potential core mineral resources that can contribute to the diversification of supply chains and the enhancement of high-tech industrial competitiveness in terms of expanding inter-Korean economic cooperation in the future and strengthening the industrial base after unification.

question: The Korean Peninsula is a peninsular country surrounded by the sea on three sides. However, due to South-North division, it is unable to utilize its geographical advantages as a peninsular country. Which of the following is NOT an advantage of being a peninsular country if the South and the North are unified?

choices.text: Creating economic and cultural synergies through the expansion of exchange with neighboring countries
Being able to travel to Europe by train
Maritime trade becomes more developed due to the severance of land routes
Being able to become a hub for international logistics

explanation: The Korean Peninsula is a peninsular country surrounded by the sea on three sides, having the geographical advantage of being able to connect the ocean and the continent simultaneously. However, currently, the land route directly connecting to the continent is blocked due to South-North division, preventing the full utilization of this advantage. If inter-Korean relations improve in the future, land transport networks with continental countries such as China and Russia will be connected, leading to more active economic and cultural exchange, and direct movement to the Eurasian continent via the Trans-Siberian Railway will become possible through connection with continental railway networks. Additionally, if a complex logistics system utilizing both sea and land simultaneously is established, it can grow into a logistics hub of Northeast Asia.
